tossups.question: An artist’s apprentice blames one of these beings for alterations to Murillo’s paintings in an anonymous story often titled for them but originally called “The Unknown Painter.” As evidence of the existence of these beings, William Seabrook cited Article 246 of their homeland’s criminal code in his travelogue The Magic Island. Feeding these beings salt will restore them to their original form, so that they are no longer barred from entry into (*) lan Guinée. Bizango societies are implicated in the creation of these beings, which involves the removal of ti bon ange (“tee bone onzh”). A bokor used a concoction including tetrodotoxin from pufferfish to make Clairvius Narcisse into one of these beings, per Wade Davis’s account in his book The Serpent and the Rainbow. For 10 points, name these mindless, undead beings from the folklore of Haiti.
tossups.question_sanitized: An artist's apprentice blames one of these beings for alterations to Murillo's paintings in an anonymous story often titled for them but originally called "The Unknown Painter." As evidence of the existence of these beings, William Seabrook cited Article 246 of their homeland's criminal code in his travelogue The Magic Island. Feeding these beings salt will restore them to their original form, so that they are no longer barred from entry into (*) lan Guinee. Bizango societies are implicated in the creation of these beings, which involves the removal of ti bon ange ("tee bone onzh"). A bokor used a concoction including tetrodotoxin from pufferfish to make Clairvius Narcisse into one of these beings, per Wade Davis's account in his book The Serpent and the Rainbow. For 10 points, name these mindless, undead beings from the folklore of Haiti.

tossups.question: Warning: Reasonably specific answer required. Original language or translation acceptable. Miles, Panizza, and Reis used this phrase to title their book on "Inflation and the Great Recession." Alien life contrarian Avi Loeb replaced the final word in this phrase with "deviated" to defend his views about ‘Oumuamua (“OO-moo-ah-moo-ah”) in his recent book Extraterrestrial. This phrase is written underneath a nail in a portrait supposedly painted by Bartolomé Esteban Murillo that really dates to the 19th century. This phrase may have been said to Melchior Inchofer before its speaker returned to his house in Arcetri. Several historians have argued that it would have been (*) suicidal to have uttered this phrase in 1633 by a man whose Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems had been placed on the Vatican's Index of Prohibited Books. For 10 points, name this phrase supposedly uttered by Galileo after recanting his belief about the Earth rotating around the Sun.
tossups.answer: "And yet it moves" [or "Eppur si muove" or "and still it moves"]
tossups.answer_sanitized: "And yet it moves" [or "Eppur si muove" or "and still it moves"]
tossups.question_sanitized: Warning: Reasonably specific answer required. Original language or translation acceptable. Miles, Panizza, and Reis used this phrase to title their book on "Inflation and the Great Recession." Alien life contrarian Avi Loeb replaced the final word in this phrase with "deviated" to defend his views about 'Oumuamua ("OO-moo-ah-moo-ah") in his recent book Extraterrestrial. This phrase is written underneath a nail in a portrait supposedly painted by Bartolome Esteban Murillo that really dates to the 19th century. This phrase may have been said to Melchior Inchofer before its speaker returned to his house in Arcetri. Several historians have argued that it would have been (*) suicidal to have uttered this phrase in 1633 by a man whose Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems had been placed on the Vatican's Index of Prohibited Books. For 10 points, name this phrase supposedly uttered by Galileo after recanting his belief about the Earth rotating around the Sun.

tossups.question: An artist who was so “sick of” this genre that he wished to play his viola da gamba departed from it late in his career with so-called “fancy pictures” inspired by Bartolomé Murillo (“moo-REE-yo”). Godfrey Kneller used a 36-by-28-inch format for paintings in this genre that were named for the Kit-Cat Club. Peter Lely helped pioneer opulent “swagger” paintings in this genre. The lecture series Discourses on Art sought to elevate this genre to the status of history painting by applying the (*) “grand manner” style. An artist left an unpainted patch in a painting that fused landscape with this genre and includes a dog looking at a rifle. English paintings in this genre influenced by the work of Anthony van Dyck include one kept at the Huntington Library and one named for Jonathan Buttall’s blue outfit. For 10 points, Joshua Reynolds and Thomas Gainsborough mostly painted in what genre that depicts people?
tossups.answer: portraits [or portraiture; accept group portraits or swagger portraits or kit-cat portraits; reject “self-portraits”] (The paintings are Mr. and Mrs. Andrews and The Blue Boy.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: portraits [or portraiture; accept group portraits or swagger portraits or kit-cat portraits; reject "self-portraits"] (The paintings are Mr. and Mrs. Andrews and The Blue Boy.)

Godfrey Kneller used a 36-by-28-inch format for paintings in this genre that were named for the Kit-Cat Club. Peter Lely helped pioneer opulent "swagger" paintings in this genre. The lecture series Discourses on Art sought to elevate this genre to the status of history painting by applying the (*) "grand manner" style. English paintings in this genre influenced by the work of Anthony van Dyck include one kept at the Huntington Library and one named for Jonathan Buttall's blue outfit. For 10 points, Joshua Reynolds and Thomas Gainsborough mostly painted in what genre that depicts people?

tossups.question: This city’s water supply begins with the Zongo and Tuni glaciers. The Miradores of Laikakota (lai-ka-KO-ta) and Killi Killi (KI-yi KI-yi) overlook this city’s skyline. Business in this city slows down for its hour of Akullicu (a-ku-YI-ku) practiced by its indigenous population. This city’s colorful Sopocachi (so-po-KA-chi) Market is located near its Parque Montículo (PAR-kay mon-TI-ku-lo). Calle Jaen (KA-ye HA-en) is the only remnant of its colonial district. A plateau above this city juxtaposes its wealthy La Ceja (la SE-ha) neighborhood with the adjacent adobe-brick slums of El Alto. This city’s Roosevelt Central Park is bisected by the Choqueyapu (cho-ke-YA-poo) River, which later flows through its country’s Valley of the Moon. Its extremely steep Calle Sagárnaga (KA-ye sa-GAR-na-ga) contains a marketplace patrolled by Aymara seers known as yatiri, the witches’ market. The Plaza Murillo (PLA-za mu-RI-yo) is the center of its government and contains its country’s “Burnt Palace.” For 10 points, name this highest capital city in the world, the administrative capital of Bolivia.
tossups.question_sanitized: This city's water supply begins with the Zongo and Tuni glaciers. The Miradores of Laikakota (lai-ka-KO-ta) and Killi Killi (KI-yi KI-yi) overlook this city's skyline. Business in this city slows down for its hour of Akullicu (a-ku-YI-ku) practiced by its indigenous population. This city's colorful Sopocachi (so-po-KA-chi) Market is located near its Parque Monticulo (PAR-kay mon-TI-ku-lo). Calle Jaen (KA-ye HA-en) is the only remnant of its colonial district. A plateau above this city juxtaposes its wealthy La Ceja (la SE-ha) neighborhood with the adjacent adobe-brick slums of El Alto. This city's Roosevelt Central Park is bisected by the Choqueyapu (cho-ke-YA-poo) River, which later flows through its country's Valley of the Moon. Its extremely steep Calle Sagarnaga (KA-ye sa-GAR-na-ga) contains a marketplace patrolled by Aymara seers known as yatiri, the witches' market. The Plaza Murillo (PLA-za mu-RI-yo) is the center of its government and contains its country's "Burnt Palace." For 10 points, name this highest capital city in the world, the administrative capital of Bolivia.

tossups.question: The trufis, a fleet of collective taxis, link the center of this city to smaller suburbs such as Obrajes (“oh-BRA-hays”) and Barrio Rosasani. Neil Armstrong named a valley of aroused sandstone mountains south of this city after the barren landscape of the moon. An elevated transportation system constructed by Doppelmayr in this city provides a free travelling stage for emerging musicians called “Do Re Mi Teleférico.” Yatiri aid clients in constructing cha’llas (“CHA-yass”), sometimes out of dried llama fetuses, in this city’s (*) Witches’ Market. The Plaza Murillo is located in this city, which is linked to the Yungas region by a namesake “Road of Death.” The longest and highest cable car system in the world connects this city to the predominantly Aymaran city of El Alto. For 10 points, name this city that houses the seat of the Bolivian government along with Sucre.
tossups.answer: La Paz [prompt on El Alto until “witches” is read]
tossups.answer_sanitized: La Paz [prompt on El Alto until "witches" is read]
tossups.question_sanitized: The trufis, a fleet of collective taxis, link the center of this city to smaller suburbs such as Obrajes ("oh-BRA-hays") and Barrio Rosasani. Neil Armstrong named a valley of aroused sandstone mountains south of this city after the barren landscape of the moon. An elevated transportation system constructed by Doppelmayr in this city provides a free travelling stage for emerging musicians called "Do Re Mi Teleferico." Yatiri aid clients in constructing cha'llas ("CHA-yass"), sometimes out of dried llama fetuses, in this city's (*) Witches' Market. The Plaza Murillo is located in this city, which is linked to the Yungas region by a namesake "Road of Death." The longest and highest cable car system in the world connects this city to the predominantly Aymaran city of El Alto. For 10 points, name this city that houses the seat of the Bolivian government along with Sucre.

tossups.question: Veteran songster Carlos Meijia Godoy released the single “Monimbo siempre con vos” to show his support for political opposition in this country. In this country, Cardinal Miguel Obando y Bravo has been accused of using apparently biological son Roberto Rivas as a proxy for extensive political corruption. Highly expensive public sculptures known as “trees of life” have been destroyed in this country to rapturous applause. Anti-pension reform protests in this country attacked the role of vice-president and first lady Rosario (*) Murillo whose husband has maintained co-operation with ICE officials despite criticism of his government’s attacks on the Catholic church led by Mike Pence. The city of Masaya in this country is currently under control by rebels against its Sandinista government. For 10 points, name this country where student-led protests have been agitating against the government of Daniel Ortega.
tossups.question_sanitized: Veteran songster Carlos Meijia Godoy released the single "Monimbo siempre con vos" to show his support for political opposition in this country. In this country, Cardinal Miguel Obando y Bravo has been accused of using apparently biological son Roberto Rivas as a proxy for extensive political corruption. Highly expensive public sculptures known as "trees of life" have been destroyed in this country to rapturous applause. Anti-pension reform protests in this country attacked the role of vice-president and first lady Rosario (*) Murillo whose husband has maintained co-operation with ICE officials despite criticism of his government's attacks on the Catholic church led by Mike Pence. The city of Masaya in this country is currently under control by rebels against its Sandinista government. For 10 points, name this country where student-led protests have been agitating against the government of Daniel Ortega.

tossups.question: Édouard Manet and an artist from this country title a book by Geneviève Lacambre (“zhen-uv-YEV lah-COM-bruh”) and Gary Tinterow. With Battistello Caracciolo and Belisario Corenzio, an artist from this country led a “cabal” that monopolized commissions in Italy. An artist from this country depicted seven men clad in white saying grace, unaware of St. Hugh entering the refectory. An artist from this country often used a window sill to frame his still lifes, which featured strings suspending fruits and vegetables. An artist from this country painted several “vaporous” depictions of the (*) Immaculate Conception in contrast with his genre paintings like Boys Eating Grapes and Melon. This country was home to an artist that depicted a saint clad entirely in white with his arms tied up in a sharply tenebrist (“TEN-uh-brist”) martyrdom scene. Another artist from this country showed a man resting his hand on a large jug in his depiction of a waterseller, and also painted An Old Woman Frying Eggs. For 10 points, name this country of Francisco de Zurbarán, Bartolomé Murillo (“moo-REE-yo”), and Diego Velázquez.
tossups.answer: Kingdom of Spain [or Reino de España] (The book in the first line is Manet/Velázquez.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Kingdom of Spain [or Reino de Espana] (The book in the first line is Manet/Velazquez.)

tossups.question: [Note to moderator (DON’T READ THIS OUT LOUD): pronounce the word “Orixas” carefully, like ore-ICKS-uh] The first saint born in this nation was known for healing people using “paper pills” and was called Anthony of Saint Anne. One religion primarily practiced in this country was founded by a follower of Allan Kardec who at 17 years old claimed to speak to a spirit called “Father Anthony”. The primate of this nation is currently Murillo Ramos Krieger. In 2009, a nine-year old in this nation faced automatic excommunication for aborting a pair of twins, according to archbishop (*) Jose Sobrinho. The largest Marian shrine in the world is located in this nation, and is devoted to Our Lady of Aparecida. A mixture of indigenous beliefs and Catholicism in this nation created a religion which venerates ancestors as Egum and believes in spirits spelled with the letter “x,” called Orixas; that religion is Candomble. For 10 points, name this country, in which one celebrates Carnival.

The primate of this nation is currently Murillo Ramos Krieger. In 2009, a nine-year old in this nation faced automatic excommunication for aborting a pair of twins, according to archbishop (*) Jose Sobrinho. The largest Marian shrine in the world is located in this nation, and is devoted to Our Lady of Aparecida. A mixture of indigenous beliefs and Catholicism in this nation created a religion which venerates ancestors as Egum and believes in spirits spelled with the letter "x," called Orixas; that religion is Candomble. For 10 points, name this country, in which one celebrates Carnival.

tossups.question: Cotton and Murillo discovered a ditungsten paddlewheel complex with an extremely low value of this quantity, which equals the negative energy of the HOMO, according to Koopmans' theorem. Calculating the enthalpy of formation of sodium chloride requires knowing this quantity for gaseous sodium. Mulliken electronegativity is the average of electron affinity and this quantity, which generally increases from left to right and decreases from top to bottom on the periodic table. For 10 points, name this energy required to remove the outermost electron from an atom.
tossups.answer_sanitized: ionization energy [or ionization potential; or IE; or IP]
tossups.question_sanitized: Cotton and Murillo discovered a ditungsten paddlewheel complex with an extremely low value of this quantity, which equals the negative energy of the HOMO, according to Koopmans' theorem. Calculating the enthalpy of formation of sodium chloride requires knowing this quantity for gaseous sodium. Mulliken electronegativity is the average of electron affinity and this quantity, which generally increases from left to right and decreases from top to bottom on the periodic table. For 10 points, name this energy required to remove the outermost electron from an atom.

tossups.question: Bartolome Murillo’s version of this scene features a skull and a cross sitting on a ledge to the right and sees the main subject clad in red looking upwards and to the left. Caravaggio’s version of this scene shows some beads and a lamp discarded on the floor while the main subject sits with crossed legs and arms in a trance-like state. Titian executed (*) two versions of this scene, one in which the main subject is clothed and the other nude, both of which show that person with crossed arms looking upwards and to the right. The best known version of this scene shows a skull on the main subject’s lap and features a candle flame reflected in a mirror; that version was painted by Georges de la Tour. For 10 points, name this scene showing a female saint who was cleansed of seven demons.
tossups.question_sanitized: Bartolome Murillo's version of this scene features a skull and a cross sitting on a ledge to the right and sees the main subject clad in red looking upwards and to the left. Caravaggio's version of this scene shows some beads and a lamp discarded on the floor while the main subject sits with crossed legs and arms in a trance-like state. Titian executed (*) two versions of this scene, one in which the main subject is clothed and the other nude, both of which show that person with crossed arms looking upwards and to the right. The best known version of this scene shows a skull on the main subject's lap and features a candle flame reflected in a mirror; that version was painted by Georges de la Tour. For 10 points, name this scene showing a female saint who was cleansed of seven demons.

bonuses.leadin: Depending on the amount of tenebrism used, paintings by an artist from this country can be divided into “cool,” “warm,” and “vaporous” styles. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Depending on the amount of tenebrism used, paintings by an artist from this country can be divided into "cool," "warm," and "vaporous" styles. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this birth country of the artist of Boys Eating Grapes and Melon. A portrait of a pope by another artist from this country provides the basis for a “study” of a ghastly figure by Francis Bacon.

Early in his career, Bartolomé Esteban Murillo (“moor-REE-yoh”) based his art style on the works of this tenebrist nicknamed the “Spanish Caravaggio.” This Seville-based artist painted a lamb with its legs tied up in the work Agnus Dei.
Like Murillo, this artist of Portrait of Innocent X (“the tenth”) lightened his use of tenebrism later in his career. This court painter also created Las Meninas.
parts_sanitized: Name this birth country of the artist of Boys Eating Grapes and Melon. A portrait of a pope by another artist from this country provides the basis for a "study" of a ghastly figure by Francis Bacon.

Early in his career, Bartolome Esteban Murillo ("moor-REE-yoh") based his art style on the works of this tenebrist nicknamed the "Spanish Caravaggio." This Seville-based artist painted a lamb with its legs tied up in the work Agnus Dei.
Like Murillo, this artist of Portrait of Innocent X ("the tenth") lightened his use of tenebrism later in his career. This court painter also created Las Meninas.
answers: Diego Velázquez [or Diego Rodríguez de Silva y Velázquez]
answers_sanitized: Spain [or Kingdom of Spain or Reino de Espana]
Diego Velazquez [or Diego Rodriguez de Silva y Velazquez]

bonuses.leadin: Guido Reni’s Saint Michael the Archangel Defeats Satan appears at the entrance to a church dedicated to this subject, which features a crypt with ornamental art crafted from the bones of Capuchin monks. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Guido Reni's Saint Michael the Archangel Defeats Satan appears at the entrance to a church dedicated to this subject, which features a crypt with ornamental art crafted from the bones of Capuchin monks. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this scene. Bartolomé Murillo (“moo-REE-yoh”) painted many “vaporous” depictions of this scene, including one in which the central figure stands on a crescent moon while surrounded by tons of cherubs.

Reni’s Saint Michael was commissioned by this family. A Pietro da Cortona fresco in this family’s Palazzo references the three bees on their coat of arms, and they name a statue of a faun with splayed legs leaning back.

Late in his career, Reni began producing rushed works to compensate for his addiction to this activity. Two men, one of whom reaches behind his back into his belt, cheat a third man at this activity in a Caravaggio (“care-uh-VAH-joh”) painting.

answers: Immaculate Conception [accept Santa Maria della Concezione dei Cappuccini]
Barberini [accept Allegory of Divine Providence and Barberini Power; accept Palazzo Barberini; accept Barberini Faun]

bonuses.leadin: A child of this country's president played the Duke of Mantua in an extravagant 2018 production of Rigoletto staged in its capital. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country where protesters have uprooted the expensive Trees of Life planted by its Vice President and First Lady, Rosario Murillo.

Daniel Ortega's son Laureano headed a failed plan to build one of these infrastructure projects in Nicaragua with Chinese funds. An expansion for another of these projects completed in 2016 added support for vehicles classified as "New Panamax."

Nicaraguan Vice President Sergio Ramírez won the Cervantes Prize in 2017. Another winner, Elena Poniatowska, gained fame for covering this 1968 massacre of students in Mexico's Plaza de las Tres Culturas.
answers: a canal between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans [or a ship canal]
answers_sanitized: Nicaragua [or Republic of Nicaragua; or Republica de Nicaragua]
a canal between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans [or a ship canal]

bonuses.leadin: Francisco Pacheco argued that this artist’s genre scenes incorporating still-lifes, such as one in which a water seller hands a boy a glass of water with a fig in it, “found the true imitation of nature.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist, who depicted a boy watching an old woman fry eggs in another of his paintings drawn from everyday life. This artist later painted a group portrait of the Infanta Margarita and her servants in Las Meninas.

The Waterseller of Seville and Old Woman Frying Eggs are examples of these paintings, also a specialty of Francisco de Zurbarán. This term refers to Spanish paintings set in kitchens or taverns, or still-lifes of food itself.

An impoverished pair of these people eat grapes and melon in one of Bartolomé Murillo’s many genre paintings of them. Chardin often painted these people playing games, as in his depiction of one of them blowing soap bubbles.
answers: Diego Velázquez [or Diego Rodríguez de Silva y Velázquez]
answers_sanitized: Diego Velazquez [or Diego Rodriguez de Silva y Velazquez]

parts: Name this Baroque artist, noted for their many depictions of the Immaculate Conception. This artist’s works depicting contemporary life include The Young Beggar and The Little Fruit Seller.
Murillo was influenced by this artist, the leading light of Philip IV’s court. The only surviving nude by this artist of The Surrender of Breda is the Rokeby Venus.

Both Murillo and Velázquez came from this city. Velázquez created three versions of a painting titled for a Waterseller of this city, while Murillo painted a St Isidore for this city’s cathedral, one of the largest in the world.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about clocks whose hands run counterclockwise, for 10 points each.
parts: As part of a campaign to move away from European norms, an anti-clockwise clock was installed in the legislative building in this capital city, which overlooks Plaza Murillo. Its other attractions include a Witches’ Market that sells herbal medicine.

Paolo Uccello (“oo-CHELL-oh”) painted an anti-clockwise clock with twenty-four divisions for this city’s cathedral, whose dome was designed by Filippo Brunelleschi. This Tuscan city was the hometown of Dante.

This city’s Jewish Town Hall includes separate clocks with Roman and Hebrew numerals, the latter running anti-clockwise. However, the Orloj (“OR-loy”) astronomical clock in this city’s Old Town Square runs normally.
answers: La Paz, Bolivia [or Nuestra Señora de La Paz; or Chuqi Yapu; or Chuquiago]

packet.name: N. MSU A + Carleton University A + Rochester A

answers_sanitized: La Paz, Bolivia [or Nuestra Senora de La Paz; or Chuqi Yapu; or Chuquiago]

bonuses.leadin: Genre paintings by this artist include one of two women at a window, in which one of the women hides her mouth beneath her shawl as if to conceal her snickering. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter who died after falling from scaffolding while working on his Mystic Marriage of St. Catherine for a Capuchin church. He also painted two beggar boys eating grapes and a melon.

Murillo hailed from, and spent most of his career in, this Andalusian city, painting many pictures of its poor. Murillo also painted this city's St Isidore reading from a book, and Diego Velázquez painted a water carrier from here.
Critics use this term for the soft tones and colors of Murillo's religious paintings, such as his Immaculate Conception, which features putti blending into the background like haze.

bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Genre paintings by this artist include one of two women at a window, in which one of the women hides her mouth beneath her shawl as if to conceal her snickering. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this painter who died after falling from scaffolding while working on his Mystic Marriage of St. Catherine for a Capuchin church. He also painted two beggar boys eating grapes and a melon.

Murillo hailed from, and spent most of his career in, this Andalusian city, painting many pictures of its poor. Murillo also painted this city's St Isidore reading from a book, and Diego Velazquez painted a water carrier from here.
Critics use this term for the soft tones and colors of Murillo's religious paintings, such as his Immaculate Conception, which features putti blending into the background like haze.

bonuses.leadin: This leader of the ominously-named “Cabal of Naples” painted that city’s patron saint, Januarius, emerging unscathed from a furnace. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this artist who painted a jolly-looking Clubfooted Boy and multiple canvases of St. Bartholomew being flayed. While working in Italy, he was given a diminutive nickname referencing his nationality.

Ribera, and the later Murillo and Zurbaran, were the foremost Spanish painters in this period of art characterized by dramatic tension and lighting effects. Caravaggio is the best-known Italian painter from this period from around 1600 to 1750.
This term, from the Italian for “murky”, refers to an extreme version of chiaroscuro pioneered by Caravaggio. Paintings in this style contrast a predominantly dark canvas with patches of light.
answers: Jusepe de Ribera [or Jose de Ribera; or Giuseppe de Ribera; or Lo Spagnoletto; or The Little Spaniard]
answers_sanitized: Jusepe de Ribera [or Jose de Ribera; or Giuseppe de Ribera; or Lo Spagnoletto; or The Little Spaniard]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This leader of the ominously-named "Cabal of Naples" painted that city's patron saint, Januarius, emerging unscathed from a furnace. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Identify this artist who painted a jolly-looking Clubfooted Boy and multiple canvases of St. Bartholomew being flayed. While working in Italy, he was given a diminutive nickname referencing his nationality.

Ribera, and the later Murillo and Zurbaran, were the foremost Spanish painters in this period of art characterized by dramatic tension and lighting effects. Caravaggio is the best-known Italian painter from this period from around 1600 to 1750.
This term, from the Italian for "murky", refers to an extreme version of chiaroscuro pioneered by Caravaggio. Paintings in this style contrast a predominantly dark canvas with patches of light.

bonuses.leadin: A Baroque painter from this country was known for painting street urchins, as in The Young Beggar, and was influenced by a religious artist from this country who painted a white-clad Saint Serapion with arms bound by ropes. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country, also home to the realist painter of The Clubfoot who painted a saint looking horrified just before getting hoisted onto a cross in The Martyrdom of Saint Philip.
Murillo was a contemporary of this other Spanish painter, who depicted himself looking outward at the viewer while working on a canvas in his giant painting Las Meninas, or The Maids of Honor.
Las Meninas is located in this Madrid art museum - the largest in Spain - along with many other works by Velazquez such as The Surrender at Breda.
answers: Kingdom of Spain [those painters are Bartolome Murillo, Francisco de Zurbaran, and Jose de Ribera]
answers_sanitized: Kingdom of Spain [those painters are Bartolome Murillo, Francisco de Zurbaran, and Jose de Ribera]

Murillo was a contemporary of this other Spanish painter, who depicted himself looking outward at the viewer while working on a canvas in his giant painting Las Meninas, or The Maids of Honor.
Las Meninas is located in this Madrid art museum - the largest in Spain - along with many other works by Velazquez such as The Surrender at Breda.

bonuses.leadin: In this man’s version of the Immaculate Conception, Mary stands atop a pile of angels. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter also known for his depictions of children and urchins, such as The Young Beggar.
Murillo was a contemporary of this other Spanish painter, who worked at the court of Philip IV and painted Las Meninas and Los Borrachos.
Inspired by a visit to Italy, this historical Velazquez work depicts Justinus van Nassau handing some keys to Ambrogio Spinola after a battle of the 80-Years War.
answers_sanitized: The Surrender of Breda or La rendicion de Breda

Inspired by a visit to Italy, this historical Velazquez work depicts Justinus van Nassau handing some keys to Ambrogio Spinola after a battle of the 80-Years War.
